title: Text evidence (RL)

questions.question_content:
Escape rooms offer a thrilling combination of mental challenges and physical adventure. These  experiences require teams to escape from a themed room by using hidden clues. Picture yourself trapped in an ancient Egyptian tomb with only an hour to escape before the walls cave in. Imagine yourself  a desert island to find buried treasure before pirates find it first. These are the kinds of exciting scenarios you might encounter in an escape room. 

To successfully escape from an escape room, team members must use their diverse skills to solve tricky puzzles. A team might, for example, need to identify three numbers that will allow them to open a combination safe. The clue to the first number might require them to solve a math problem, while the second clue relies on trivia knowledge, and the third clue involves a complex word puzzle. The team’s math experts can tackle the first clue, while the  get to work on clue number three. Good communication is essential: team members need to quickly exchange ideas to work through complex problems. 

If you and your friends are great communicators with varied talents and perspectives, you might want to plan a visit to a local escape room. 


**Which detail from the text best shows *why* having diverse skills can help teams solve escape room puzzles?**

note: KA serves 1/4 questions to anonymous users. Passage and sample question captured for skeleton generation.
